title: 🤖 Copilot Adoption [Edu]
description: Tracks GitHub Copilot seat provisioning, activation rates, and daily active user trends across the organization.

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green) or synthetic seed data (amber).

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE('Live: ' || (SELECT value FROM app_config WHERE key = 'org') || '/' || (SELECT value FROM app_config WHERE key = 'repo'), 'Not yet synced') AS "Data Source"

options.content:
# 🤖 Copilot Adoption & Usage

**Supplementary Metrics — Copilot Seat Utilisation**

## What it Measures
This dashboard tracks how broadly and actively GitHub Copilot is being used across your organisation. It covers seat allocation, activation rates, and 28-day active usage — giving you a clear picture of Copilot adoption health.

## Why it Matters
Seat utilisation directly affects ROI. Inactive seats represent wasted spend and missed productivity opportunities. Understanding *who* is using Copilot and *how often* helps managers target enablement efforts and coaches identify developers who may need onboarding support.

## How to Interpret
| Metric | Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|---|
| **Seat Utilisation Rate** | ≥80% active in 28d | <50% — investigate blockers or low awareness |
| **Inactive Seats (>14d)** | Close to 0 | Rising count — reach out to inactive users |
| **Feature Adoption** | Multiple surfaces used | Single surface — expand use-case awareness |

panels.description: Copilot Adoption & Usage — seat utilisation, active users, and feature adoption.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Total Seats" FROM copilot_seats

panels.title: Total Seats - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Total count of all rows in copilot_seats at sync time, regardless of activity.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Snapshot, not history** — this endpoint returns who has a seat right now. Seats added or removed between syncs are not tracked historically.
- **Multi-org gap** — if your company has multiple GitHub orgs, each requires a separate API call. Seats are not auto-aggregated across orgs.
- **403 = 0 silently** — the sync server logs a warning on 403 but continues. The table stays empty and this panel shows 0.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Historical seat assignment changes.
- Billing or cost information per seat.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Total Seats" FROM copilot_seats
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Total Seats.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Active Seats (28d)" FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.title: Active Seats (28d) - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` → `last_activity_at` field

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Count of seats where last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'. 'Activity' means any Copilot interaction (suggestions, chat, completions).
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Broad activity definition** — last_activity_at is updated by any Copilot interaction, including rejected suggestions. A developer who dismissed one suggestion 27 days ago counts as 'active'.
- **28-day window is hardcoded** — this is NOT your Grafana time picker. It is always relative to NOW().
- **last_activity_at granularity** — in some API versions this field is a date (not datetime). Multiple activities on the same day are indistinguishable.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Frequency of activity within the 28-day window.
- Which Copilot features were used (completions vs chat vs CLI).

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Active Seats (28d)" FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Active Seats (28d).

panels.title: Seat Utilization Rate (%) - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Ratio of active seats (28d) to total seats. Uses PostgreSQL COUNT(*) FILTER syntax.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Two different time contexts** — numerator uses rolling NOW()-28d; denominator uses total seats at sync time. A recently-provisioned seat inflates the denominator before the user becomes active.
- **Utilization ≠ value** — 90% utilization with very low acceptance rates means Copilot is used but not found helpful.
- **Newly provisioned seats deflate rate** — a seat provisioned yesterday for a new employee counts in the denominator immediately.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Whether highly-utilized seats generate accepted suggestions.
- Which seats are underutilized and why.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days')::numeric * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1
) AS "Seat Utilization Rate (%)" FROM copilot_seats
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Seat Utilization Rate (%).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Inactive Seats (>14d)"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '14 days'
   OR last_activity_at IS NULL

panels.title: Inactive Seats (>14d) - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Seats where last_activity_at is older than 14 days OR NULL (never active). Useful for identifying seats to reclaim.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **NULL last_activity_at included** — seats that have never been used are NULL, included via OR IS NULL.
- **14-day threshold is arbitrary** — a developer on a 2-week vacation is flagged as inactive.
- **Seat reassignment has a billing lag** — GitHub only allows reassignment at end of billing cycle. Identifying inactive seats ≠ reclaiming them immediately.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Why seats are inactive (vacation, project pause, dissatisfaction).
- Billing impact of reclaiming vs keeping inactive seats.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Inactive Seats (>14d)"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '14 days'
   OR last_activity_at IS NULL
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Inactive Seats (>14d).

panels.title: Daily Active Users

targets.rawSql:
SELECT day AS time, daily_active_users AS "Daily Active"
FROM copilot_enterprise_daily
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) → NDJSON → `daily_active_users` field | Tables: `copilot_enterprise_daily`

panels.title: Daily Active Users - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) → NDJSON → `daily_active_users` field

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_enterprise_daily`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Time series of daily_active_users from the org-level 28-day report. Each row in copilot_enterprise_daily is one day.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **NDJSON response pattern** — this endpoint returns a `download_links` envelope. The sync server must: (1) call the API endpoint, (2) extract `download_links[0].url` from the response, (3) fetch that signed S3/CDN URL WITHOUT GitHub auth headers, (4) read the response body and parse each line as a separate JSON object.
- **28-day rolling window** — the /latest endpoint always covers the most recent 28 days. Data older than 28 days requires the `organization-1-day` endpoint with a specific `?day=YYYY-MM-DD` parameter.
- **Unique users per day, not interactions** — a user who made 100 suggestions counts the same as one who made 1.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- How many suggestions each active user generated.
- Daily active users by editor, language, or repository.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT day AS time, daily_active_users AS "Daily Active"
FROM copilot_enterprise_daily
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Daily Active Users.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT last_activity_editor AS "Editor", COUNT(*) AS "Seats"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_editor IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

panels.title: Last Active Editor Distribution - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` → `last_activity_editor` field

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Bar chart of seat counts grouped by last_activity_editor (e.g., 'vscode', 'neovim', 'jetbrains').
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Last editor, not primary editor** — this records the most recently used editor. A developer who uses VS Code daily but tried the CLI once will show as whichever they used last.
- **Editor string includes version** — some API responses report 'vscode/1.80.0' vs 'vscode', creating separate bars for the same editor at different versions.
- **NULL excluded** — seats where the user has never been active (NULL last_activity_editor) are filtered out by the WHERE clause.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Primary editor usage across the team.
- Multi-editor users who switch between IDEs.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT last_activity_editor AS "Editor", COUNT(*) AS "Seats"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_editor IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Last Active Editor Distribution.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  assignee_login AS "Developer",
  last_activity_at AS "Last Active",
  last_activity_editor AS "Editor",
  plan_type AS "Plan"
FROM copilot_seats
ORDER BY last_activity_at DESC

panels.title: Seat Activity Recency - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Table of all seat holders sorted by last_activity_at descending, showing login, last active date, editor, and plan type.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **No activity history** — only the single most-recent activity date is stored per seat. Activity frequency over time is not captured.
- **plan_type field** — shows Copilot plan (Individual / Business / Enterprise), which affects feature entitlements.
- **Sync cadence affects recency** — if syncs run daily, last_activity_at is accurate to ±1 day. Infrequent syncs produce stale recency data.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Activity history or trend over time per seat.
- Which specific Copilot features each developer uses.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  assignee_login AS "Developer",
  last_activity_at AS "Last Active",
  last_activity_editor AS "Editor",
  plan_type AS "Plan"
FROM copilot_seats
ORDER BY last_activity_at DESC
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Seat Activity Recency.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats

panels.title: Copilot Seats Loaded - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Row count of copilot_seats table. Used as a data health indicator alongside 'Last Successful Sync'.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Silent 403 failure** — if the sync server receives a 403 on the seats endpoint, it logs a warning and continues. The table stays at 0 rows. A 'successful' sync job ≠ seats data was loaded.
- **TRUNCATE + INSERT every sync** — unlike PRs and deployments (incremental), copilot_seats is fully replaced on every sync. A 403 leaves it empty.
- **Proxy for sync health** — use this alongside 'Last Successful Sync' to confirm data freshness; check server logs if this shows 0.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Whether seat data is accurate or stale.
- Breakdown by team or repository.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Copilot Seats Loaded.

panels.title: Last Successful Sync

targets.rawSql: SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'

panels.description: Source: Internal sync_jobs table (no GitHub API call) | Tables: `sync_jobs`

panels.title: Last Successful Sync - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source

Internal sync_jobs table (no GitHub API call)

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`sync_jobs`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

MAX(finished_at) from sync_jobs where status='success'. Shows when the last complete sync run finished.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **'Successful' sync ≠ all data loaded** — a sync job completes with status='success' while individual fetchers (e.g., copilot seats) fail silently. Always check records_synced per resource.
- **Timestamps in UTC** — finished_at is stored in UTC. Grafana displays in browser timezone; a sync at 00:00 UTC appears as yesterday in UTC-5 timezones.
- **sync_jobs is append-only** — each sync adds a row and the table grows indefinitely. No automatic pruning.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Whether all resources were successfully fetched (check records_synced column in the job record).
- Failure reason if the last job has status other than 'success'.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Last Successful Sync.
